explanation: The input requirement states that the system must connect to the pressurized gas supply (oxygen and medical air) and accept pressures up to 5.2 bar. From the available atomic propositions, 'airSupplyConnected' indicates connection to the air supply, and 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' represents that gas supply pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. We want a property that essentially says that whenever the air supply is connected, the system must only accept or allow gas pressures less than or equal to 5.2 bar at all times (globally, whenever air supply is connected, pressure must be below or equal to 5.2 bar). This is a safety requirement, so we use the template G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3) with bool_exp1 as 'airSupplyConnected' and bool_exp3 as 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2'.

The natural language requirement states that the system must connect to the pressurized gas supplies (oxygen and medical air) and accept pressures up to 5.2 bar. The atomic proposition 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' directly refers to the gas supply pressure condition, i.e., the system's input pressure must always be less than or equal to 5.2 bar. The most suitable translation enforces this universally: at all times, the property 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' must hold. This is captured by template 7 (G bool_exp3), which ensures that under all circumstances, the pressurized gas supply should not exceed 5.2 bar, aligning with the safety and connection condition in the requirement.

The requirement states that the system shall connect to a pressurized gas supply of oxygen and medical air, and that it should accept (i.e., not exceed) pressures up to 5.2 bar. The available atomic proposition most relevant to this is 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2', which indicates that the pressure of the gas supply (oxygen and medical air) is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. To capture the requirement that the system must always accept such pressures, we want to enforce as an invariant that the pressure remains within this bound. Therefore, the property should always (globally) hold that the supply pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. Template 7 ('G bool_exp3') is the most suitable, plugging in 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' as bool_exp3.

The natural language requirement states that the system shall connect to pressurized gas supplies (oxygen and medical air) and accept inlet pressures up to 5.2 bar. Within the provided atomic propositions, the only directly relevant AP is 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2', which indicates that the pressure of the gas supply (oxygen and medical air) is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. The requirement does not refer to a particular event or conditional, but rather to a global constraint: at all times, the system should only accept (i.e., function with) pressures up to 5.2 bar. This is best formalized as a safety property, using the template 'G bool_exp3', which means 'it is always the case that the pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar'. This captures the requirement that at no time should the system accept a pressure above this threshold.

The natural language requirement relates to the system being able to connect to (1) a pressurized gas supply for oxygen and (2) a pressurized gas supply for medical air, and being able to accept a pressure up to 5.2 bar. In the provided atomic propositions, 'airSupplyConnected' represents that medical air has been supplied, and 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' indicates the gas supply pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. No single atomic proposition mentions specifically that the system is connected to the oxygen supply, so the best available abstraction is to represent that, whenever the system is connected to the air supply, it must also satisfy the pressure condition (i.e., that the pressure of gas supply—whether for oxygen or medical air—is less than or equal to 5.2 bar). Therefore, the property 'airSupplyConnected -> gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' must hold globally (at all times during operation), indicating that if the air supply is connected, then the gas supply pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. This is best represented by template 30. No additional boolean logic required.

The requirement specifies that the system must connect to the pressurized gas supply of both oxygen and medical air and must accept pressures up to 5.2 bar. The atomic proposition 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' directly corresponds to the pressure being at or below 5.2 bar, and 'airSupplyConnected' implies that the system is connected to medical air, while 'powerConnected' implies the connection to the (breathing) circuit, but for simplicity, the most relevant proposition for the connection to the air supply is 'airSupplyConnected'. To ensure the system always fulfills this requirement, it should always be the case (globally) that if the air supply is connected, then the pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. Thus, the best fitting LTL template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', with bool_exp1 as 'airSupplyConnected' (connected to supply of air/oxygen), and bool_exp3 as 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' (pressure up to 5.2 bar).

The natural language requirement specifies that the system must connect to both oxygen and medical air supplies and must be able to accept gas supply pressures up to 5.2 bar. Among the atomic propositions provided, 'airSupplyConnected' (indicates connection to air supply) and 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' (pressure of gas supply of O2 and medical air ≤ 5.2 bar) are most relevant. The requirement is predominantly a global safety and design constraint ('shall accept pressures up to 5.2 bar'), suggesting that this condition must always be satisfied. Using template 8 (G (! bool_exp3)), we can express that it is always the case that the pressure does not exceed 5.2 bar, i.e., globally it is not true that the gas supply pressure is above 5.2 bar. Here, the boolean expression for violation would simply be '!gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2', but since we want to specify correct pressure, we instead use 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' directly in a positive property (could use G(bool_exp3)), but template 8 inverts: 'G (! (!gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2))' = 'G (gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2)'. In this template, bool_exp3 is '!gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' and the template is 'G (! bool_exp3)'. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement states that the system must connect to a pressurized gas supply (oxygen and medical air) and accept pressures up to 5.2 bar. Within the provided atomic propositions, 'airSupplyConnected' corresponds to being connected to the medical air supply, and 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' expresses that the incoming gas supply pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. Thus, the property should state that whenever the system is connected to the air supply, the pressure must be within the tolerated maximum of 5.2 bar. The most representative LTL template is an invariance (globally) of the implication: if 'airSupplyConnected' holds, then it must also be the case that 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' holds.

The input states the system shall connect to (and accept) gas supply of oxygen and medical air with pressures up to 5.2 bar. In the given atomic propositions, the only relevant one that matches this physical requirement is 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2', which is true when gas supply pressure is less than or equal to 5.2 bar. To capture a safety property that always holds (the system never accepts a pressure greater than 5.2 bar), the best matching template is 'G bool_exp3' (template 7). Here, bool_exp3 is 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2', expressing that at all times, the input (gas supply pressure) is less than or equal to 5.2 bar, matching the specified bound in the requirement. No other atomic propositions reference system connection of supply or acceptance of pressure. N_DURATION is left unused in this template.

The property specifies that the system must be connected to both oxygen and medical air gas supplies and must accept input gas pressures up to 5.2 bar. This requirement is best captured by requiring that, always (globally), it holds that 'airSupplyConnected' (air supply) is true, and that 'gbps_lessThanOrEqual_5_2' (gas supply pressure less than or equal to 5.2 bar) is also true. While there is no distinct proposition for O2 supply connection, we infer that safe operation for inspiratory gas supply implies both air and O2 sources (or at minimum, supports the ceiling pressure), so we use the available relevant propositions. This requirement does not depend on an event, so a global (always) invariant is chosen.
